title: sideroxylon reclinatum subsp. austrofloridense
intro: Sideroxylon reclinatum subsp. austrofloridense, also known as the Black Ironwood or Black Iron Tree, is a subspecies of the Sideroxylon reclinatum tree, native to the southwestern regions of Western Australia. The Black Ironwood has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits associated with the Black Ironwood include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce pain and inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The herbal preparations of the Black Ironwood typically involve drying and crushing the leaves or bark, which can then be made into teas, infusions, or topical applications.
